steps.title: Spot the contrasting signal
steps.content: The word “however” in the second sentence introduces a critical contrast. The initial claim is that species belong to clades predating Gondwana's split ~80 million years ago. Pillon et al.'s study then reports a crown age of only 7.3 million years for the Geissois clades—directly at odds with the 80-million-year timeline.

steps.title: Match the structure to the answer
steps.content: Choice D accurately captures this structure—it describes an idea put forward, then presents results incompatible with that idea. Choices A and C infer claims the passage never makes (A about explaining the discrepancy, C about researchers reconsidering), and B mischaracterizes the study's purpose. That matches choice D — D is correct.
